content: You are a precise coding agent working in a small FastAPI repository. Use the provided tools to inspect files, make minimal edits, and run the tests. Think step by step before acting.

content.text: Add a `GET /tickets/{ticket_id}` endpoint to this FastAPI app that returns the matching ticket, or HTTP 404 if no ticket has that id. The repository layer already exposes `TicketRepository.get_ticket`; add a `get_ticket` method to `TicketService` that delegates to it, then wire the route in `app/main.py`. Read the relevant files first, make the edits, and run the test suite to confirm everything passes. Keep changes minimal.

     1	"""Business logic layer for ticket operations."""
     2	
     3	from __future__ import annotations
     4	
     5	from app.models import Ticket, TicketCreate, TicketListResponse
     6	from app.repository import TicketRepository
     7	
     8	
     9	class TicketService:
    10	    """Orchestrates ticket operations between routes and the repository."""
    11	
    12	    def __init__(self, repository: TicketRepository) -> None:
    13	        self._repository = repository
    14	
    15	    def list_tickets(self, *, include_archived: bool = False, search: str | None = None) -> TicketListResponse:
    16	        """Return tickets filtered by archive status and optional search term."""
    17	        tickets = self._repository.list_tickets()
    18	
    19	        if search:
    20	            lowered = search.lower()
    21	            tickets = [
    22	                ticket
    23	                for ticket in tickets
    24	                if lowered in ticket.title.lower() or lowered in ticket.description.lower()
    25	            ]
    26	
    27	        if not include_archived:
    28	            tickets = [t for t in tickets if not t.archived]
    29	
    30	        return TicketListResponse(items=tickets, total=len(tickets))
    31	
    32	    def create_ticket(self, payload: TicketCreate) -> Ticket:
    33	        """Validate and persist a new ticket from the given payload."""
    34	        ticket = Ticket(
    35	            id=0,
    36	            title=payload.title.strip(),
    37	            description=payload.description.strip(),
    38	            customer_email=payload.customer_email.strip(),
    39	            priority=payload.priority,
    40	        )
    41	        return self._repository.create_ticket(ticket)
    42	
    43	    def archive_ticket(self, ticket_id: int) -> Ticket | None:
    44	        """Archive a ticket by ID. Returns the updated ticket or None."""
    45	        return self._repository.archive_ticket(ticket_id)
    46

Here's the result of running `cat -n` on /swe_repo/app/repository.py:
     1	"""In-memory ticket repository providing CRUD persistence."""
     2	
     3	from __future__ import annotations
     4	
     5	from app.models import Ticket, TicketPriority, TicketStatus
     6	
     7	
     8	class TicketRepository:
     9	    """Stores tickets in memory with seed data for demonstration purposes."""
    10	    def __init__(self) -> None:
    11	        self._tickets: list[Ticket] = [
    12	            Ticket(
    13	                id=1,
    14	                title="Checkout button fails on Safari",
    15	                description="Customers report the purchase CTA does nothing on Safari 17.",
    16	                customer_email="ops@northwind.example",
    17	                priority=TicketPriority.high,
    18	                status=TicketStatus.investigating,
    19	            ),
    20	            Ticket(
    21	                id=2,
    22	                title="Exported CSV omits assignee",
    23	                description="Analytics exports should include the current owner column.",
    24	                customer_email="bi@acme.example",
    25	                priority=TicketPriority.medium,
    26	                status=TicketStatus.open,
    27	            ),
    28	            Ticket(
    29	                id=3,
    30	                title="Password reset email arrives twice",
    31	                description="Duplicate notifications are confusing support contacts.",
    32	                customer_email="support@globex.example",
    33	                priority=TicketPriority.medium,
    34	                status=TicketStatus.open,
    35	            ),
    36	            Ticket(
    37	                id=4,
    38	                title="Deprecated webhook endpoint still listed in docs",
    39	                description="Archive this issue after the docs refresh lands.",
    40	                customer_email="docs@initech.example",
    41	                priority=TicketPriority.low,
    42	                status=TicketStatus.archived,
    43	            ),
    44	        ]
    45	        self._next_id = 5
    46	
    47	    def list_tickets(self) -> list[Ticket]:
    48	        """Return defensive copies of all stored tickets."""
    49	        return [ticket.model_copy() for ticket in self._tickets]
    50	
    51	    def create_ticket(self, ticket: Ticket) -> Ticket:
    52	        """Persist a new ticket, assigning a unique ID."""
    53	        created = ticket.model_copy(update={"id": self._next_id})
    54	        self._next_id += 1
    55	        self._tickets.append(created)
    56	        return created.model_copy()
    57	
    58	    def _get_ticket(self, ticket_id: int) -> Ticket | None:
    59	        """Look up a ticket by ID. Returns the live reference or None."""
    60	        for ticket in self._tickets:
    61	            if ticket.id == ticket_id:
    62	                return ticket
    63	        return None
    64	
    65	    def get_ticket(self, ticket_id: int) -> Ticket | None:
    66	        """Look up a ticket by ID. Returns a defensive copy or None."""
    67	        ticket = self._get_ticket(ticket_id)
    68	        return ticket.model_copy() if ticket is not None else None
    69	
    70	    def archive_ticket(self, ticket_id: int) -> Ticket | None:
    71	        """Mark a ticket as archived. Returns a copy or None if not found."""
    72	        ticket = self._get_ticket(ticket_id)
    73	        if ticket is None:
    74	            return None
    75	        ticket.status = TicketStatus.archived
    76	        return ticket.model_copy()
    77

     1	"""Pydantic domain models and request/response schemas for the issue tracker."""
     2	
     3	from __future__ import annotations
     4	
     5	from datetime import datetime, UTC
     6	from enum import StrEnum
     7	
     8	from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, computed_field
     9	
    10	
    11	class TicketPriority(StrEnum):
    12	    """Allowed priority levels for a ticket."""
    13	
    14	    low = "low"
    15	    medium = "medium"
    16	    high = "high"
    17	
    18	
    19	class TicketStatus(StrEnum):
    20	    """Lifecycle states a ticket can occupy."""
    21	
    22	    open = "open"
    23	    investigating = "investigating"
    24	    resolved = "resolved"
    25	    archived = "archived"
    26	
    27	
    28	class Ticket(BaseModel):
    29	    """Full ticket representation returned by the API."""
    30	
    31	    id: int
    32	    title: str
    33	    description: str
    34	    customer_email: str
    35	    priority: TicketPriority = TicketPriority.medium
    36	    status: TicketStatus = TicketStatus.open
    37	    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(UTC))
    38	
    39	    @computed_field
    40	    @property
    41	    def archived(self) -> bool:
    42	        """Derived from status; True when status is archived."""
    43	        return self.status == TicketStatus.archived
    44	
    45	
    46	class TicketCreate(BaseModel):
    47	    """Request body schema for creating a new ticket."""
    48	
    49	    title: str
    50	    description: str
    51	    customer_email: str
    52	    priority: TicketPriority = TicketPriority.medium
    53	
    54	
    55	class TicketListResponse(BaseModel):
    56	    """Paginated list response wrapping ticket items."""

    58	    items: list[Ticket]
    59	    total: int
    60

         return self._repository.archive_ticket(ticket_id)

diff --git a/app/main.py b/app/main.py

 @app.get("/hello")
@@ -49,6 +49,15 @@ def create_ticket(payload: TicketCreate) -> Ticket:
     return service.create_ticket(payload)
 
 
+@app.get("/tickets/{ticket_id}", response_model=Ticket)
+def get_ticket(ticket_id: int) -> Ticket:
+    """Return a single ticket by ID, or 404 if not found."""
+    ticket = service.get_ticket(ticket_id)
+    if ticket is None:
+        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail="Ticket not found")
+    return ticket
+
+
 @app.patch("/tickets/{ticket_id}/archive", response_model=Ticket)
 def archive_ticket(ticket_id: int) -> Ticket:
     """Archive a ticket by ID. Returns 404 if the ticket does not exist."""
